2026-03-25T22:26:54.1155356Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1155782Z ✓ $ # Check what pnpm build actually does at root cat /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js/turbo.json | head -30
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1156186Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1157599Z Agent: Good. So the authoritative build command is `pnpm turbo build --filter=<package>... --token 1`. The CONTRIBUTING.md uses `pnpm turbo build --filter=<package>...` (without `--token 1`), while CLAUDE.md uses `pnpm turbo build --filter=<package>... --token 1`. Since CONTRIBUTING.md is considered more "official" documentation, I should follow `pnpm turbo build --filter=<package>...` without the `--token 1` flag for the documentation changes (since `--token 1` is used for CI remote cache access).
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1159137Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1159300Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1160500Z Agent: Actually, thinking about this more—the `--token 1` flag in CLAUDE.md is specifically for enabling remote cache read access in CI environments, which most contributors wouldn't have set up. So for general documentation aimed at developers, the version without `--token 1` from CONTRIBUTING.md makes more sense.
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1161515Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1164579Z Agent: Now I'm scanning through the other documentation files to identify all the inconsistencies. I'm seeing that `golden-testing-commands.md` uses `npm run test:*` commands when everything else uses `pnpm`, and there are similar issues with `pnpm build` that should be `pnpm turbo build`. Let me go through the files systematically to catch all of these. I'm continuing to scan through the remaining documentation files for similar issues—there are several more instances of `pnpm build --filter=...` that need updating to `pnpm turbo build --filter=...` across the Swagger quickstart, performance testing, and code generation guides. I also noticed that one file references an invalid npm package `@pnpm` which doesn't actually exist, so I should flag that as a potential error to investigate further.

2026-03-25T22:26:54.1250892Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1251295Z Agent: So `TestInfo` is an alias for `VitestTestContext`, which is the `context` parameter that vitest passes to `beforeEach`. So the correct pattern is:
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1251835Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1252159Z Agent: ```typescript
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1252449Z Agent: import type { TestInfo } from "@azure-tools/test-recorder";
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1252760Z Agent: // or just:
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1253045Z Agent: import { Recorder } from "@azure-tools/test-recorder";
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1253353Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1253524Z Agent: // In test:
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1253732Z Agent: beforeEach(async (context) => {
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1253993Z Agent:   recorder = new Recorder(context);
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1254237Z Agent: });
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1254398Z Agent: ```

2026-03-25T22:26:54.1362011Z Agent: So confirmed: `pnpm build --filter=@azure/storage-blob...` will run `npm run clean && dev-tool run build-package && dev-tool run extract-api` in each matched package. This doesn't handle building dependencies first. 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1362766Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1363282Z Agent: The correct command is `pnpm turbo build --filter=@azure/storage-blob...` which tells turbo to build the package and all its dependencies in the right order.
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1363861Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1364242Z Agent: So the docs that use `pnpm build --filter=...` are all wrong - they should use `pnpm turbo build --filter=...`.
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1364678Z Agent:

2026-03-25T22:26:54.1372525Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1372795Z Agent: ### Issue 1 — Wrong build command (line 132–136)
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1373069Z Agent: **Quote:**
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1373254Z Agent: ```
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1373497Z Agent: pnpm install && pnpm build --filter=<package-name>...
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1373851Z Agent: > pnpm build --filter=@azure-rest/purview-datamap...
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1374138Z Agent: ```
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1375040Z Agent: **Problem:** `pnpm build --filter=<pkg>...` runs each package's local `build` script (e.g. `dev-tool run build-package`) without turbo orchestration. It does **not** build transitive dependencies first. `CONTRIBUTING.md` explicitly prescribes `pnpm turbo build --filter=<pkg>...`.
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1375970Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1376243Z Agent: **Correct:** `pnpm turbo build --filter=@azure-rest/purview-datamap...`
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1376583Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1376742Z Agent: ---
2026-03-25T22:26:54.1376898Z Agent:

Use this after making file edits to submit them for review and merging. The PR will be created from the current branch with your committed changes. For code review comments on an existing PR, use create_pull_request_review_comment instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 pull request(s) can be created. Title will be prefixed with \"[docs] \". Labels [\"documentation\" \"automated\"] will be automatically added.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["title","body"],"properties":{"title":{"type":"string","description":"Concise PR title describing the changes. Follow repository conventions (e.g., conventional commits). The title appears as the main heading."},"body":{"type":"string","description":"Detailed PR description in Markdown. Include what changes were made, why, testing notes, and any breaking changes. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading."},"branch":{"type":"string","description":"Source branch name containing the changes. If omitted, uses the current working branch."},"labels":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"},"description":"Labels to categorize the PR (e.g., 'enhancement', 'bugfix'). Labels must exist in the repository."},"draft":{"type":"boolean","description":"Whether to create the PR as a draft. Draft PRs cannot be merged until marked as ready for review. Use mark_pull_request_as_ready_for_review to convert a draft PR. Default: true."},"repo":{"type":"string","description":"Target repository in 'owner/repo' format. Required when changes are in a subdirectory checkout (e.g., 'repos/repo-a/'). Must be in the allowed-repos list. If omitted, uses the repository at the workspace root."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_tool","description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["reason"],"properties":{"tool":{"type":"string","description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["message"],"properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing')."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":[],"properties":{"data_type":{"type":"string","description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters)."},"context":{"type":"string","description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}}]}}

to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. \nPossible options: \n 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.\n 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.\n 3. get_status - Get combined commit status of a head commit in a pull request.\n 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.\n 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.\n 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.\n 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. 2026-03-25T22:26:54.3397299Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-25T22:25:57.571578721Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Summary\n\nReviewed all 29 markdown files in `documentation/` against the current codebase and fixed the following categories of inconsistencies:\n\n---\n\n### 1. Build commands missing `turbo` (`pnpm build --filter` → `pnpm turbo build --filter`)\n\n**Files:** `linting.md`, `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`, `RLC-Swagger-quickstart.md`, `steps-after-generations.md`, `writing-performance-tests.md`\n\n`pnpm build --filter=\u003cpkg\u003e...` only runs the package's local `build` script without Turborepo orchestration — it does **not** build transitive dependencies first. `CONTRIBUTING.md` explicitly prescribes `pnpm turbo build --filter=\u003cpkg\u003e...`.\n\n---\n\n### 2. `pnpm update` → `pnpm install`\n\n**Files:** `RLC-Swagger-quickstart.md`, `steps-after-generations.md`\n\n`pnpm update` upgrades versions in the lockfile (potentially breaking things), while `pnpm install` is the correct command to refresh workspace dependencies without changing versions.\n\n---\n\n### 3. `pnpm -F {./}... build` → `npx turbo build`\n\n**File:** `golden-testing-commands.md`\n\nUpdated the \"build from inside package directory\" command to use the Turborepo-native form recommended in `CONTRIBUTING.md`.\n\n---\n\n### 4. `npm run test:*` → `pnpm run test:*`\n\n**Files:** `golden-testing-commands.md`, `writing-performance-tests.md`\n\nAll user-facing test commands replaced `npm run` with `pnpm run`. This repo enforces pnpm as the package manager via a `preinstall` script.\n\n---\n\n### 5. Outdated mocha/chai test code examples → vitest\n\n**File:** `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`\n\nThe test code examples (both RLC `recordedClient.ts` and HLC `monitor.spec.ts`) still used the old mocha/chai testing framework, which was replaced by vitest as of `@azure-tools/test-recorder` v4.0.0. Fixed:\n\n- Removed `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` (two occurrences)\n- Removed `import { assert } from \"chai\"`\n- Added `import type { TestInfo } from \"@azure-tools/test-recorder\"` for the recorder helper parameter type\n- Changed `beforeEach(async function (this: Context)` → `beforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e`\n- Changed `new Recorder(this.currentTest)` → `new Recorder(ctx)`\n- Added `import { assert, describe, it, beforeEach, afterEach } from \"vitest\"`\n- Added `.js` extensions to local imports per ESM module requirements\n\n---\n\n### 6. Deprecated `dev-tool customization apply-v2` → `apply`\n\n**File:** `migrating-to-typespec.md`\n\n`apply-v2` is deprecated and emits `\"⚠️ 'dev-tool customization apply-v2' is deprecated and will be removed soon. Use 'dev-tool customization apply' instead.\"` (confirmed in `common/tools/dev-tool/src/commands/customization/apply-v2.ts`).\n\n---\n\n### 7. `npm run` → `pnpm run` in user-facing shell instructions\n\n**File:** `migrating-to-typespec.md`\n\nReplaced `npm run generate:client`, `npm run build`, `npm run test` in Step 6, Step 11, and the \"Ongoing Development\" section with `pnpm run` equivalents.\n\n---\n\n### 8. `npm install -g @pnpm` → `npm install -g pnpm`\n\n**File:** `Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md`\n\n`@pnpm` is not a valid npm package name. The correct package is `pnpm`.\n\n---\n\n### 9. Missing `/` in shell command\n\n**File:** `resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md`\n\n```diff\n- git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml\n+ git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml\n```\n\nThe `git add` command was missing the path separator between the repo root and `pnpm-lock.yaml`, causing the command to silently fail (trying to stage a file literally named `\u003crepo-root\u003epnpm-lock.yaml`).\n\n---\n\n### 10. EOL Node.js versions in example\n\n**File:** `run-your-azure-ts-js-samples-with-multiple-node-versions.md`\n\nUpdated example `--node-versions \"12,14\"` to `\"20,22\"`. Node 12 and 14 have both been EOL since 2022–2023. The repo's `package.json` requires Node ≥ 20.\n\n---\n\n### 11. Stale hardcoded commit-SHA links\n\n**File:** `writing-performance-tests.md`\n\nReplaced a link pinned to a specific old commit SHA pointing to `sdk/storage/perf-tests/storage-blob/README.md` (a path that no longer exists) with the current `main` branch URL at the correct path `sdk/storage/storage-blob-perf-tests/README.md`.\n","branch":"docs/consistency-fixes-2025-03-25","draft":true,"title":"docs: fix inconsistencies in documentation/ folder"},"name":"create_pull_request"}}}

2026-03-25T22:26:54.3700296Z Reviewed all 29 markdown files in `documentation/` against the current codebase and fixed the following categories of inconsistencies:
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3700301Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3700375Z ---
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3700384Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3700681Z ### 1. Build commands missing `turbo` (`pnpm build --filter` → `pnpm turbo build --filter`)
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3700743Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3701197Z **Files:** `linting.md`, `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`, `RLC-Swagger-quickstart.md`, `steps-after-generations.md`, `writing-performance-tests.md`
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3701202Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3702096Z `pnpm build --filter=<pkg>...` only runs the package's local `build` script without Turborepo orchestration — it does **not** build transitive dependencies first. `CONTRIBUTING.md` explicitly prescribes `pnpm turbo build --filter=<pkg>...`.
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3702102Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3702178Z ---
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3702183Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3702315Z ### 2. `pnpm update` → `pnpm install`
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3702320Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3702502Z **Files:** `RLC-Swagger-quickstart.md`, `steps-after-generations.md`
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3702506Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3703012Z `pnpm update` upgrades versions in the lockfile (potentially breaking things), while `pnpm install` is the correct command to refresh workspace dependencies without changing versions.
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3703023Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3703096Z ---
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3703100Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3703252Z ### 3. `pnpm -F {./}... build` → `npx turbo build`
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3703257Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3703364Z **File:** `golden-testing-commands.md`
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3703368Z 
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3703458Z Updated the "build from inside pack
2026-03-25T22:26:54.3703533Z ...
